items.summary: A coroner has found paramedics failed to meet standards of care for a man who died from cardiac arrhythmia while being restrained by police.

items.title: Bellamy pinpoints Storm's downfall as Broncos achieve 10-year first
items.summary: Storm coach Craig Bellamy identifies the area in which he believes his side let themselves down, as the Broncos celebrate a rare win in Melbourne.

items.title: Federal government approves 1,695 gas wells in rural Queensland
items.summary: A major multinational gas venture has just secured federal approval to ramp up extraction in rural Queensland — locking in operations for the next 55 years.

items.title: Epworth releases details into hospital review following endometriosis allegations
items.summary: A South Australian health commissioner will lead the review of clinical governance at Epworth Hospital, after allegations a top gynaecologist performed unnecessary operations to remove endometriosis.

items.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Japan coach talks favouritism ahead of final against Matildas
items.summary: Japan's coach says determining a favourite for the Women's Asian Cup final between his team and Australia is a matter for bees and flies.

items.title: Dorothy Day House raided by police over 'From the River to the Sea' banner
items.summary: Officers raid Dorothy Day House in Greenslopes over a banner with the slogan "From the River to the Sea", which is now a prohibited expression in Queensland.

items.title: Alana King stakes claim for T20 World Cup recall with dominant performance against West Indies
items.summary: Alana King gives Australia's selectors a timely reminder of the quality she possesses, helping secure a win against the West Indies.

items.title: Trump has postponed his meeting with Xi, but China is not wasting any time on it
items.summary: Donald Trump has postponed a meeting with Xi Jinping because he is busy with Iran and the Middle East — and that's good news for China.

items.title: One Nation candidate Aoi Baxter wanted in the UK for failing to attend court on sexual touching charge
items.summary: South Australian One Nation candidate Aoi Baxter has a warrant for his arrest in the UK after he failed to show up to court to face a charge of sexually touching a woman without consent.

items.title: Ashton Hurn lives in hope of becoming SA's premier after election
items.summary: Polls predict the Liberals are heading for a wipe-out in South Australia on Saturday, but party leader Ashton Hurn says she is still working with the goal of becoming the state's leader rather than analysing what went wrong.

items.title: Australia falls short of top 10 in World Happiness Report for second year
items.summary: The 2026 World Happiness Report links heavy social media use to a sharp decline in young people's wellbeing, with teenage girls in English-speaking countries and Western Europe among the most affected.

items.title: Unmasking Banksy is missing the point of his work. What's so wrong with a little mystery?
items.summary: Does Banksy's success mean we are more entitled to demand he pull his mask off, show his face? Does it matter that he says his anonymity is core to his art?

items.title: Teenager Moise Kouame makes history with win over Zachary Svajda at ATP Masters Miami Open
items.summary: Teenager Moise Kouame steals the spotlight as Australians Alexei Popyrin and James Duckworth crash out of the multi-million-dollar Miami Open.

items.title: NT cattle station Aroona sells to Argentinian family for $44 million
items.summary: An Argentinian family has expanded its portfolio of NT cattle stations with the purchase of Aroona Station.

items.title: What to watch, read and listen to this weekend: New James Blake and a sun-drenched crime drama
items.summary: This weekend go down the rabbit hole with a podcast about a Canadian cult or discover James Blake's latest sonic salve.

items.title: When girls don't feel comfortable or confident in their uniforms it can cause them to drop out of sport
items.summary: Studies in Australia and overseas show a big percentage of girls drop out of sport when they become teenagers. One of the contributing factors is the clothes they have to wear.

items.title: How the 'four right blokes' turned the ABC's coverage of the VFA into must-watch TV for footy fans
items.summary: As Aussie Rules was forced to reckon with a national competition, four men helped make the VFA must-watch TV on the ABC for Victorian footy fans.

items.title: The ABC is investigating aged care again and we want to hear from you
items.summary: We're launching an investigation asking you — staff, insiders, those navigating the aged care system and their supporters — to help the ABC understand if the government's promise of reform has been delivered.

items.title: Video shows news crew narrowly avoiding Israeli strike in southern Lebanon
items.summary: An English-language Russian state broadcaster has accused Israel of deliberately attacking its news crew in southern Lebanon. IDF says it will investigate.

items.title: White House meeting with Japan's PM turns awkward as Trump jokes about Pearl Harbor
items.summary: When he was asked why the US did not notify allies like Japan ahead of the strikes on Iran, Donald Trump made a quip drawing a parallel with the Japanese air raid in 1941.

items.title: Iran war drives up cost of living in Asia, hits crucial tech industries
items.summary: The Middle East war triggers everything from fuel rationing in Sri Lanka to a helium supply crisis for Asia's crucial semiconductor industries.

items.title: Historic flour mill wall in Mount Gambier will be destroyed despite community opposition
items.summary: The remnants of a 140-year-old historic flour mill will be demolished to make way for a housing development despite a regional community calling for the city's history to be preserved.

items.title: Albanese, Burke shouted at as anger erupts at Eid prayers at mosque
items.summary: An event to mark the end of Ramadan was disrupted by a protest against the prime minister and home affairs minister, who were visiting the mosque in Western Sydney.

items.title: How to choose sheets and bed linen that last as long as possible
items.summary: Shopping for sheets can be overwhelming and expensive. This is what textile experts say to look out for.

items.title: What's a library worth? Report puts dollar figure on 'invaluable' institutions
items.summary: Every dollar invested in the State Library of NSW is worth $4.25 in community benefits, according to a new report commissioned by the library.

items.title: Fuel and freight woes from Middle East conflict could end some carrot farms in WA, warns industry body
items.summary: Shipping disruptions due to conflict in the Middle East mean Western Australian carrot growers have lost access to their main customer.

items.title: Townsville truckie wants action on fuel as Queensland prices hit record high
items.summary: Almost everything arrives in outback Australia by truck. Truckies say record-high fuel prices will have to be passed on.

items.title: Urgent calls for flood mitigation in Alice Springs, as insurance premiums skyrocket
items.summary: A shock flash flooding event in Alice Springs last month saw more than 100 millimetres of rain fall on the catchment in a matter of hours. The deluge has reignited calls for urgent action on flood mitigation in the outback town, as insurance companies calculate the risk with eye-watering premiums.

items.title: AFL investigates after Hawthorn's Nick Watson accuses Sydney fans of throwing drink bottle
items.summary: Hawthorn's Nick Watson says Sydney Swans supporters threw drink bottles at him after the Hawks defeated the Swans on Thursday night.

items.title: Rare Rymer apples found on NSW Southern Tablelands farm
items.summary: The Rymer variety of cooking apple, once believed to have died out, has been found on a rural property in the NSW Southern Tablelands.

items.title: As it happened -- Major bank says oil price could soar to $US150
items.summary: The CBA says there is a "strong likelihood" of the oil price reaching $US120‑150 per barrel.

items.title: Developer Chris Pinzone dropped from Paul Pisasale corruption probe, embroiled in farm payments dispute
items.summary: Developer Chris Pinzone, who was central to former Ipswich mayor Paul Pisasale's corruption case, has been pursued in a number of civil cases.

items.title: Government to continue subsidising Australia's two remaining oil refineries — as it happened
items.summary: The federal government will continue subsidising Australia's only two remaining oil refineries amid the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Maggie Beer brings aged care nutrition project to outback renal health hub
items.summary: Culinary icon Maggie Beer and her team are working with an Alice Springs organisation that provides dialysis and aged care on Indigenous homelands.

items.title: Sydney independent service station, taxi drivers hit hard by rising petrol and diesel prices
items.summary: The fuel crisis is squeezing independently owned service stations, with some being forced to sell fuel at above-average prices, even though their margins are near zero or below cost.

items.title: We might have just found where Australia's red line is with Donald Trump
items.summary: The war in the Middle East is heaping pressure on Australia's relationship with the US and we could be about to see if that comes with consequences.

items.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
items.summary: The ABC's Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran is in Beirut and providing daily updates on the Middle East war.

items.title: Australian Opal Centre at Lightning Ridge runs out of money before construction is complete
items.summary: The Australian Opal Centre in Lightning Ridge risks becoming a "white elephant" as construction indefinitely stalls and an auditor raises concerns about the organisation's viability.

items.title: Raiders prop Josh Papali'i facing NRL suspension, glass shatters at Canberra Stadium injuring coach
items.summary: Canberra Raiders prop Josh Papali'i is facing a one-match suspension for a high tackle during his side's 14-10 loss to the Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs on Thursday night.

items.title: Jim Chalmers is trying to use the current crisis as a springboard for reform
items.summary: If the treasurer is serious about budget repair the government has to avoid the inevitable pressure for more cost-of-living relief which will only grow as many families' budgets become unmanageable.

items.title: Rideshare and delivery drivers rethink gig economy amid sky-high petrol prices
items.summary: Rideshare and delivery platforms face mounting pressure to bump up rates for drivers as they struggle with skyrocketing fuel prices.

items.title: How is the Middle East war affecting Australian superannuation funds?
items.summary: Experts are urging Australians to stay calm and weather the storm as the effects of the war in the Middle East reverberate through financial markets.

items.title: Migrants say new laws stopping temporary migration from war-torn countries 'a slap in the face'
items.summary: Migrants and human rights advocates say new laws stopping temporary migration from designated countries will keep families apart and make Australia's immigration system less predictable and fair.

items.title: Prime minister's department requests modelling for gas and coal tax to shield Australia from impact of war
items.summary: The prime minister's department has requested options to impose a new tax on gas giants to help shield Australians from the economic shock of the Middle East war.

items.title: Rise in women woodchoppers prompts introduction of new female events
items.summary: Like her father, grandfather and great grandfather before her, woodchopping is part of Kallie's family legacy.

items.title: How award-winning pianist Andrea Lam learned to let go of perfection
items.summary: She made her debut with Sydney Symphony Orchestra at 13, but don't call Andrea Lam a piano prodigy. The award-winning pianist shares how she's kept kicking career goals without losing her love of the music.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: History-making Oscars moments, a royal visit and fans storm the NRL field
items.summary: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

items.title: BTS's new comeback album, concert, Netflix documentary: What you need to know
items.summary: K-pop supergroup BTS makes a comeback from mandatory military service today with a new album, concert and documentary. Here's why that's a big deal.

items.title: China cracks down on illegal fentanyl trade after Trump delays Beijing meeting
items.summary: The country is one of the world’s highest producers of the chemicals used to produce the synthetic opioid in America.

items.title: Iranian deputy foreign minister labels US 'terrorists' and issues warning to Australia
items.summary: Iranian deputy foreign minister Esmaeil Baghaei accuses the US of "terrorist acts" and issues a warning to Australia about being on the wrong side of history.

items.title: Dogs hold on to down Canberra in brutal wet-weather battle
items.summary: It was far from easy, but Canterbury have held on for a narrow win over the Raiders in torrential conditions in Canberra.

items.title: Thursday Finance with David Chau
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Great Gunston gets Hawks home at the MCG
items.summary: Hawthorn finishes over the top of an understrength Sydney side to knock off the ladder-leaders at the MCG, thanks to a four-goal haul from Jack Gunston.

items.title: Artist Ben Quilty makes case for culture in a room focused on roads
items.summary: One of Australia's most acclaimed artists brought humour and frustration to a council meeting weighing commerce against culture.

items.title: Health insurance premiums to hike as much as 25 per cent, CHOICE analysis shows
items.summary: Some Australians with private health insurance will face premium increases of as much as 25 per cent from next month, according to new analysis from CHOICE.

items.title: Albanese hopeful about end of war against Iran as budget looms
items.summary: Perhaps the prime minister was hoping US President Donald Trump was tuning in to Triple M when he claimed that the attack on Iran had now achieved its aims, and offered an off-ramp for the war to end.

items.title: Inside Lizzy Hoo's wild life escapades: music, Mongolia and the moments that shaped her
items.summary: Comedian Lizzy Hoo loves an adventure, which is how she ended up at a music festival outside Ulaanbaatar watching her new favourite band.

items.title: How boosting vaccinations could prevent meningitis outbreak in Australia
items.summary: A growing outbreak of meningococcal B and meningitis has killed two young people in the UK as the number of infections climb. Here's why infectious disease professor Paul Griffin thinks Australia should be worried.

items.title: Family who conceived through IVF at Royal North Shore Hospital seeks DNA testing
items.summary: A family is pursuing DNA testing after hearing about an IVF mix-up at the same Sydney fertility clinic used to conceive its baby.

items.title: Kangaroo Island considers introducing 'last cat policy' in bid to eradicate feral cats
items.summary: Kangaroo Island Council is considering banning the introduction of all cats to the island, in a move the mayor is calling the "last cat policy". If successful, the ban would make the South Australian island one of the world's biggest inhabited islands to be free of cats.

items.title: What Doesn't Kill You | Ghanim Al Shnen
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Iran will not boycott FIFA World Cup, but seeks to get United States-based matches moved
items.summary: Iran has no intention of boycotting the FIFA World Cup, but is seeking to have its group stage matches moved out of the United States.

items.title: Iran war leaves Japan and South Korea on edge, as US moves assets to Middle East
items.summary: As the Iran war stretches on there are mounting concerns in the Asia-Pacific that a protracted conflict, and a distracted White House, could threaten peace in the region.

items.title: Unemployment rate rises to 4.3 per cent, but rate hike fears persist amid inflation concerns
items.summary: Australia's unemployment rate rose to 4.3 per cent in February, up from 4.1 per cent, in seasonally adjusted terms.

items.title: Israel hits Iran South Pars facilities in world's largest natural gas field
items.summary: The Israeli bombing of Iranian facilities in the South Pars gas field and Iran's retaliation targeting energy infrastructure across the Persian Gulf, mark a significant point of escalation in the US-Israeli war with Iran.

items.title: Prolonged Middle East war and AI profitability 'downgrades' are major risks for economy, RBA says
items.summary: Interest rates, war in the Middle East and AI advancements are expected to hit the Australian economy, but the RBA says we are prepared.

items.title: Albanese unveils new national fuel supply taskforce to steer response to Middle East war
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has named Anthea Harris, a former chief of Australia's energy regulator, as the national coordinator of the taskforce.

items.title: Botanist gave early warning on rehab difficulties for troubled Griffin Coal's Collie mine site
items.summary: The WA government insists Griffin Coal's obligations have not changed, but changes to its mining agreement could allow the company to walk away from rehabilitating its Collie mine site.

items.title: Who in the Iranian leadership has been killed?
items.summary: A growing list of Iran's top leadership has been reportedly killed in ongoing strikes by Israel and the United States.
